Diana D. Parnell, lllD
Trustee, Marin Healthcare District
Cosmetic & Laser Surgery Institute
1l-oo S. Eliseo Drive, Suite 2
Greenbrae, California 
94904
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-95-408
Dear Dr. Parnell:
This is in response to your request for advice regarding the
conflict-of-j-nterest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
rra"lrr 1 1 as they pertain to your position as a member of the Marin
Healthcare District.
OUESTTONS
The following questions pertained to a vote on the Sutter/CHS
merger. The vote occurred on December L9, 1-995, and you did not
participate. 
You requested advice regarding your ability to vote
to allow Marin General Hospital ([],[GHrr), Marin Health System
(rrrcIs'r), and Marin Home Care (rrlff{Crr) to:
l-. Become part of a Sutter/CHS Obligated Group?
2. Roll-up existing MGH/MHS/IfiIC revenue bonds to the Sutter/
CHS Obligated Group?
3. A1low MGH to borrow unl j-mited amounts of money by
participating in future revenue bond financing involving UhGH/}'{JJS/
MIIC without District approval which is presently required when it
involves amounts in excess of 1Og of the operating budget?
L Government Code Sections 81OOO-91015. A1I statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulatj-ons, Sections
18000-L8995.
428 J Srneer, Sutre 800, Secner-aeNro, CertnonNta 95814 (916) 322-5660


File No. A-9 5-4 08
Page 2
2 tt ir not clear from
source of income to You
the hospital.
this fact r{hether the hospital vrould be a
if you see a dermatology consultation j-n
4. consent on the participation of MGH in the trExcess cash
Transfer Program.rr where money frorn the District would be able to
be transferred out of the District?
CONCLUSI ON
Since your questions relate to issues of past conduct, we are
unable to piovide advice regarding these questions' Hov'ever, we
wiif proviie general guidance with respect to future decisions.
FACTS
You were Publicly elected to the Marin Hospital District, 
nov
called the Marin Heallhcare District, in November 1992' At that
timeandnow,youareanemberofthecourtesymedicalstaffof
Mirin cenerai uospital (rrMGHrr or t'hospitalrr) ' 
You have very f ew
nrofessional dealings vith the hospital and have had only one
f,;;;ia;i 
admission in the past 25 ye?rs: You see an occasional
deriatotogy consultation in the hospital, but probably no more
ah;;-a;; i6 tn."" times per vear.2
At the time of your election, the office you shared vith your
physician husband was located in a building owned 908,!Y a group
-oi'a"cto.s. 
You and your husband had outgro\''n that office space
ana-iooXea for additi-onal space for many years' You tried to
avoid leasing from the hospital because of your.position on the
Marin Healthiare District Board. This was difficult 
because
almost all of the adequate space in the vicinity is controlled by
the hospital acting as master lessor, even though they do not own
the buildinqs.
on october 24t 1994, Parnell Medical Corporation (sublessee)
entered into a IReal Property subfeaserr with Pacific Fertility
Centers of california, Inc., (sublessor) for the lease of a
medical office in Greenbrae. california. 
It is an 18 nonth lease,
commencing on November L5, L994 and ending on May 30, 1995'
Although i.he hospital had no part in any of your negotiations,
they hive a requirement that they nust approve subleases'
As part of the above sublease, Parnell liledicat Corporation
(suntenant) also entered into a rrconsent to sub]easerr with Pacific
i"ilifit' 
iledical Center-Marin, Inc. (tenant) and Marin ceneral
Hospital (1andIord) on October 24, 7994'
Si,nce your letter requesting advice was received in our
office aftei ttre vote was taken, rre were not able to advise you on
youi que=tions. I ca11ed you to discuss your. relluest and to
infori you that we are not able to render advice on issues of past
"""a""t. 
You informed ne that you did not participate in the


File No. A-9 5 -4 08
Page 3
decisions, but would like to know about your ability to
participai.e in future decisions regardj-ng the hospital-
You al-so inforrned me that Parnell Medical corporation has a
l-imited partnership in, and you are on the board of, a
pharrnaceirtica I cornpany that may sell small quantities of
iredication to Marin General HospitaI. Hol'ever, there have not
been any sales to the hospital in the last two years'
ANALYSTS
Because you did not describe a decision that is pendingT at
the piesent time, we can only provide you with general guidance as
to tire conf I ict-of - interest provisions of the Act'
Section 87l,oo of the Act prohibj'ts any public official at any
level- of state or 1oca1 governrnent frorn nakj,ng, participating in
."Xi"g,-". 
in any way atfenPting to-use his or her official
;;;ial;" 
to inflirenc! a govern*ental -decision which will have a
ieasonably foreseeable and rnateriaf financial effect on an
economic interest.
section 87103 specifies that a public official 
has a
tinanciat interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision wilt have a materiaL financial- effect,
aistinguishable from the effect on the public generally. on the
officiil- 
or a menber of his or her irnnediate family or on:
(a) Any business entity in which the public
officiai 
has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars ($1,oo0) or more'
(b) Any real property in vhich the public
officiai 
has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars ($1,ooo) or more'
(c) Any source of incorne, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commerciat lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms avaj-Iable to the publ'ic without regard to
official 
status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value provided to,
received'by or promised to the public official
within 12 rnonths prior to the tine !'hen the
decision is made.
(d) Any business entity in which the public
orficiai is I airector, officer, partner, trustee'
"rpf"V"", or holds any position of management '
(e) Any donor of, or any internediary or.
aqent for a donor of, a gift 91. eift= aggregating
tio- n""ai"a fifty doi'Iars ($250) or more in val-ue
provided to, reclived by, or promised to the public


File No. A-9 5-4 08
Page 4
official within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is made. The amount of the value of
gifts specified by this subdivision sha11 be
edjusted biennially by the comnission to equal the
sarne amount determined by the Commission pursuant
to subdivision (d) of section 89504.
Your economic interests that we are alrare of, are as fol-lows:
1. Parnelt tledical corDoration
Pursuant to Sections 87103(a) , (c) and (d), your medical
business is considered an economic interest and you may not
participate in any decision that will- have a reasonably
ioreseelbl,e naterial f inancj-a1 effect, distinguishabl-e fron the
effect on the public generally, on your business.
2. Pharmaceutical companv
The pharmaceutical- company is also an economic interesc under
Sections eZfo: (a) , (c) and (d). Purchasers of nedications from
the pharmaceuticaL company, such as Marin General Hospital, may
also be a source of income to you. (section 87103(c) .) 
Pursuant
to section 82030(a), your income includes a pro rata share of any
incorne of a business entity in r.lhich you or your spouse have a ten
percent interest or greater.
3. Marin General HosPital
We do not have any facts to deterrnine whether the hospital is
a source of incorne to you. For example, if you see a dermatology
consul-tation in the hospj-ta1, does the hospital pay you?
Determining j,f the hospital is a source of income to you is a
crj.tical fictor in deternininq .if you may participate in decisions
which wil-l affect the hosPital.
4. ReaI ProPertv Interests
Your leasehold interest is an econonic interest. 
(section
87103(b).) Section 82033 provides that an rrinterest in real
propertytr' includes any leasehold, beneficial, or ownershi-p
intLresl in real property located in the jurisdiction 
owned
directly, inairectty, or beneficially by the official if the fair
market ialue of the interest is $1,ooo or more.3
Regnrlation L8729 (b) provides that the value of a leasehold
interesf is the amount of rent owed during a 12-nonth period'
Since you pay more than $1,ooo in a 12-month period, you have a
1easeh6ld inLerest in reaL property as defined in the Act' 
Thus,
you nay not participate in any decision that will have a
3 A nonth-to-nonth tenancy is not a leasehold interest under the
Act. (Regulation 18233. )


File No. A-95-408
Page 5
reasonably foreseeable material financial effect, distingruishable
from the effect on the public generally, on your leasehold
interest .
Foreseeabj-1ity and Materialitv
whether the financial consequences of a decision are reason-
ably foreseeabl-e at the time a governmental decision is made
deplnds on the facts of each particular case. An effect is
co-nsldered reasonably foreseeable if there is a substantial
likelihood that it will- occur. Certainty is not required'
Hoi4rever, if an effect is only a mere possibility, 
it is not
reasonably foreseeable. (In re Thorner (1975) 1 FPPC ops. l-98. )
For a foreseeable f i-nancial effect on an official's 
interests
to be disqualifying, the foreseeabLe financial effect must also be
material-. - fhe bornrnission has adoPted differing guidelines to
determine whether an effect is material, depending on the nature
of the economic interests involved and the specific circumstances
of each decision. For examPle, \.rhere an econornic interest is
directly before the Marin Healthcare District, Regulation la702.L
providei that the effect of the decision is generally deemed to be
inaterial-. (Conbs Advj-ce Letter, No. A-89-177. )
where an official's 
econornic interests are indirectly
involved in a decision, Regulations 18702.2 through L8702 ' 6 app]y,
dependinq on the economic interest invotved in the decision. With
reipect fo yor. medj-cal corporation or interest in the
phainaceutilal company, Regul-ation L8702.2 provides thresholds to
letermine materiality depending on the financial size of the
business entity. 
For exampfe, ReguJ-ation LA102.2(g) | copy
enclosed, provides for a relativel,y smal1 business that the effect
of a decision on an official's 
business is naterial if:
(1) The decision will result in an increase
or decrease in the gross revenues for a fiscal year
of $1o,oo0 or more; or
(21 The decision will result in the business
entity incurring or avoiding additional expenses or
reducing or elirninating existing expenses for a
fiscal year in the amount of l2,5oo or nore; or
(3) The decision will result in an increase
or decrease in the value of assets or liabilities
of $10, ooo or more.


File No- A-9 5-4 0 8
Page 6
With respect to your leasehold interest, Regulation \A7O2.4
provides :
The effect of a decision is material as to a
leasehoLd interest in reaf property if any of the
following applies:
(a) The decision s/il1 change the l-egaf Iy
allowable use of the feased property, and the
lessee has a right to sublease the property;
(b) It is reasonably foreseeable that the
lessee will change the actual use of the property
as a resuLt of the decision;
(c) It is reasonably foreseeable that the
decision !ri11 resul-t in a change in the actual use
of property within 300 feet of the l-eased property,
and the changed use will significantly enhance or
significantly decrease the use or enjoyment of the
leased property;
(d) The decision wil-I increase or decrease
the amount of rent for the leased property by $250
or 5 percent, whichever is greater, during any 12-
month period following the decision; or
(e) The decision r.riII result in a change in
the ternination date of the lease.
You have not posed a question regarding a decision that is
pending at this tirne. For any hospital district decision, you
rnust determine if any of your econonic interests will be
naterially affected. I hope the foregoing has provided you with
guideline to analyze confl ict-of- interests issues.
If you have any further questions regarding this matter or
future issues which roay arise, please contact ne at (916) 322-
5660.
sincerely,
Steven c. Churchwell
General Counsel
